---
title: "Laboratorio 5 - Naive Bayes"
author: "SmartStay Advisors"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r carga-datos}
# Carga y preparación de datos
load("listings_modificado.RData")

categoricas <- c("room_type", "property_type", "neighbourhood", "city",
                 "host_is_superhost", "instant_bookable", "bathroom_type")
columnas_existentes <- intersect(categoricas, names(listings))
listings[columnas_existentes] <- lapply(listings[columnas_existentes], as.factor)

if (!"price_cat" %in% names(listings)) {
  cortes <- quantile(listings$price, probs = c(0.33, 0.66), na.rm = TRUE)
  listings$price_cat <- cut(listings$price,
                             breaks = c(-Inf, cortes[1], cortes[2], Inf),
                             labels = c("Economica", "Intermedia", "Cara"),
                             include.lowest = TRUE)
  listings$price_cat <- as.factor(listings$price_cat)
}
```

```{r division-datos}
# División de datos con muestra de 10,000 observaciones
library(e1071)
library(caret)

set.seed(123)
muestra_idx <- sample(1:nrow(listings), size = 10000)
muestra     <- listings[muestra_idx, ]

n         <- nrow(muestra)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = 0.7 * n)
train     <- muestra[train_idx, ]
test      <- muestra[-train_idx, ]
```

```{r punto-1-modelo-regresion-nb}
# Punto 1: Modelo de regresión Naive Bayes
modelo_nb_reg <- naiveBayes(
  price ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train
)

pred_nb_factor <- predict(modelo_nb_reg, newdata = test)
pred_nb_reg    <- as.numeric(as.character(pred_nb_factor))
```

```{r punto-2-metricas-regresion}
# Punto 2: Métricas del modelo de regresión
rmse_nb <- RMSE(pred_nb_reg, test$price, na.rm = TRUE)
mae_nb  <- MAE(pred_nb_reg, test$price, na.rm = TRUE)
r2_nb   <- cor(pred_nb_reg, test$price, use = "complete.obs")^2

cat("RMSE:", round(rmse_nb, 2), "\n")
cat("MAE: ", round(mae_nb, 2), "\n")
cat("R2:  ", round(r2_nb, 4), "\n")
```

```{r punto-3-comparacion-regresion}
# Punto 3: Comparación con modelos anteriores
library(rpart)

modelo_lm <- lm(
  price ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train
)
pred_lm <- predict(modelo_lm, newdata = test)
rmse_lm <- RMSE(pred_lm, test$price, na.rm = TRUE)
r2_lm   <- cor(pred_lm, test$price, use = "complete.obs")^2

modelo_tree_reg <- rpart(
  price ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train, method = "anova", control = rpart.control(maxdepth = 6)
)
pred_tree <- predict(modelo_tree_reg, newdata = test)
rmse_tree <- RMSE(pred_tree, test$price, na.rm = TRUE)
r2_tree   <- cor(pred_tree, test$price, use = "complete.obs")^2

comparacion_reg <- data.frame(
  Modelo    = c("Lasso - Lab 3", "Arbol Regresion prof=6 - Lab 4",
                "Random Forest 100 arboles - Lab 4", "Naive Bayes - Lab 5"),
  RMSE_Test = round(c(4168.63, 2478.74, 2410.27, rmse_nb), 2),
  R2_Test   = round(c(0.030, 0.6648, 0.6907, r2_nb), 4)
)
print(comparacion_reg)
```

```{r punto-4-modelo-clasificacion}
# Punto 4: Modelo de clasificación Naive Bayes
train$price_cat <- as.factor(train$price_cat)
test$price_cat  <- as.factor(test$price_cat)

modelo_nb_clf <- naiveBayes(
  price_cat ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train
)
```

```{r punto-5-eficiencia-clasificacion}
# Punto 5: Eficiencia del modelo de clasificación
pred_clf <- predict(modelo_nb_clf, newdata = test)

accuracy <- mean(pred_clf == test$price_cat, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Accuracy:", round(accuracy * 100, 2), "%\n")

print(confusionMatrix(pred_clf, test$price_cat))
```

```{r punto-6-matriz-confusion}
# Punto 6: Matriz de confusión
cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_clf, test$price_cat)

print(cm$table)
print(cm$byClass)
cat("Accuracy:", round(cm$overall["Accuracy"] * 100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Kappa:   ", round(cm$overall["Kappa"], 4), "\n")

cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm$table)
colnames(cm_df) <- c("Predicho", "Real", "Frecuencia")

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Real, y = Predicho, fill = Frecuencia)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Frecuencia), size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#d9d9d9", high = "#404040") +
  labs(title = "Matriz de Confusion - Naive Bayes Clasificacion",
       x = "Clase Real", y = "Clase Predicha") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r punto-7-sobreajuste}
# Punto 7: Análisis de sobreajuste
pred_train_clf <- predict(modelo_nb_clf, newdata = train)
accuracy_train <- mean(pred_train_clf == train$price_cat, na.rm = TRUE)
accuracy_test  <- mean(pred_clf == test$price_cat, na.rm = TRUE)

cm_train <- confusionMatrix(pred_train_clf, train$price_cat)

cat("Accuracy Entrenamiento:", round(accuracy_train * 100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Accuracy Prueba:       ", round(accuracy_test  * 100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Diferencia:            ", round((accuracy_train - accuracy_test) * 100, 2), "pp\n")
cat("Kappa Entrenamiento:   ", round(cm_train$overall["Kappa"], 4), "\n")
cat("Kappa Prueba:          ", round(cm$overall["Kappa"], 4), "\n")
```
